Here are the changes I'm making to the application:

### UI/UX Specification:

1.  **Detailed View Card Update**:
    *   **Behavior**: The total quantity of an order is a primary piece of information. I'm moving it from the card's footer to the header, right before the party's name, to give it more prominence.
    *   **Visuals**: The quantity will be displayed in a distinct badge with the brand's accent color for quick scanning. The generic box icon previously used for quantity will be removed, as the new badge is more descriptive and cleans up the card footer. The other status icons (for notes, high volume, and overdue orders) will now be neatly aligned to the right side of the card footer.

2.  **Summarized View Card Update**:
    *   **Behavior**: When viewing orders grouped by party in the summarized view, it's helpful to see what items are contained within without needing to expand the card. I will now display a preview of the unique "Style-Color" combinations for that party's orders.
    *   **Visuals**: This preview will only be visible on the *collapsed* card to provide a quick summary. Once you expand the card to see the individual orders, the preview will hide to reduce redundancy and keep the interface clean. The text will be subtly styled and truncated to prevent it from overwhelming the card header.

I've implemented these changes in the `pendingorder.tsx` file. Here is the updated code:

entries.description: Updated the Detailed view to move total quantity to the header and removed the associated icon. Updated the Summarized view to show unique Style-Color pairs on collapsed party cards.

OKYH NOW AFTER SELECTING ORDER I.E USING CHECK BOX THERE IS EXPORT  CSV BTN RENAME TO EXPORT BTN

https://script.google.com/macros/s/AKfycbwC2oLSeZO6eou1zA1svKgt2mrkKrzSACnvec2iznYVVre_bw7aEAqxuIGFJQCN7W3o/exec

for this order matching logic CREATE A NEW FILE exportmatching.tsx in that file write the exporting code

**Envisioning Order Exports**

I'm now focusing on adding an "Export" button within the PendingOrders view. My goal is for this button to trigger a call to a Google Apps Script endpoint, sending the data of the selected orders. I'm exploring the best way to integrate this functionality seamlessly.


**Refining Export Mechanism**

I'm currently working to integrate the "Export" button's new functionality.  Instead of just generating a CSV, it'll POST selected order data to a Google Apps Script endpoint. I'm focusing on crafting the `exportmatching.tsx` component to handle this complex export flow. This will include base64 encoding the JSON and managing the script interaction and response. The old `export CSV` function is now obsolete as `export` is all encompassing.


**Developing Batch Processing**

My current focus is adapting the script to handle batch exporting.  I'm modifying `exportmatching.tsx` to loop through selected orders, encoding and sending each to the Google Apps Script. I'm considering using Promises to manage the asynchronous requests and provide a status update on the export progress.  I'll also implement error handling to address any failures during the process and clearly communicate them to the user.


**Designing Batch Exports**

I'm now focused on the component structure for `exportmatching.tsx`, which needs to manage batch order exports. I'll iterate through selected orders, encoding and sending each to the Google Apps Script. I'm considering integrating a progress indicator and error handling to ensure a smooth, user-friendly experience during this batch process. The UI needs to convey progress effectively, even when encountering failures. I'll need to figure out how to update the UI on completion.


**Orchestrating Batch Exports**

I'm now focusing on creating `exportmatching.tsx` to handle batch exports. This component will manage the entire export flow: iterate through selected orders, construct requests, handle base64 encoding, manage the interaction with the Google Apps Script endpoint, and display the export progress to the user. I plan to use a modal to give real-time updates. I'll need to figure out how to format my HTTP request body so it's compatible with the Google Apps Script's `e.parameter.jsonData` handling, which is based on `x-www-form-urlencoded`.


**Designing Export Modal**

I'm now focused on the component structure for `exportmatching.tsx`, which needs to manage batch order exports. Excellent idea! Consolidating multiple orders into a single export file is much more efficient. I'll update the application to handle this.

Here is the plan:

1.  **Consolidated Data Payload**: When you select multiple orders and click "Export," the app will now intelligently combine them.
    *   **Order & Party Details**: The Order Numbers and Party Names will be merged into single, comma-separated strings (e.g., "K123, K124", "Party A, Party B").
    *   **Item Aggregation**: The core of the change is that the app will scan through all items from all selected orders. If an item with the same barcode appears in multiple orders, their quantities will be summed up. This creates one master list of items with their total quantities across all selected orders.

2.  **Single Export Process**: Instead of exporting each order one-by-one, the app will now send this single, consolidated payload to the Google Apps Script.

3.  **Backend & UI**:
    *   The Google Apps Script is already set up to handle this kind of data, so no changes are needed there. It will generate a single template file with the aggregated quantities.
    *   The export progress modal has been updated. Instead of showing a list of individual orders, it will now display a single status for the entire consolidated batch, making the process cleaner and easier to track.

This approach streamlines the workflow and ensures all your selected items are neatly organized in one file. Here is the updated file:
